data.label: Conversational Retrieval QA Chain
data.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
data.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

inputParams.label: Rephrase Prompt
inputParams.name: rephrasePrompt
inputParams.description: Using previous chat history, rephrase question into a standalone question
inputParams.warning: Prompt must include input variables: {chat_history} and {question}

inputParams.label: Response Prompt
inputParams.name: responsePrompt
inputParams.description: Taking the rephrased question, search for answer from the provided context
inputParams.default:
I want you to act as a document that I am having a conversation with. Your name is "AI Assistant". Using the provided context, answer the user's question to the best of your ability using the resources provided.
If there is nothing in the context relevant to the question at hand, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure" and stop after that. Refuse to answer any question not about the info. Never break character.
------------
{context}
------------
REMEMBER: If there is no relevant information within the context, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure". Don't try to make up an answer. Never break character.

inputAnchors.label: Memory
inputAnchors.name: memory
inputAnchors.description: If left empty, a default BufferMemory will be used

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the language model
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

inputs.responsePrompt:
You are a supply chain analyst assistant. Use the context to answer 
questions about supplier performance. When comparing suppliers, 
always include Supplier_ID, Supplier_Name, and the relevant metric.
Format lists clearly. If data is insufficient to give a complete 
answer, say so.

Where youre at the very first message only that time as you respond to user a greeeting add this text "-RAG by Vighnesh Jun 26 "

this must appear only once at begining 

Context: {context}

Answer:

outputAnchors.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.label: ConversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

inputParams.label: Additional Metadata
inputParams.name: metadata
inputParams.description: Additional metadata to be added to the extracted documents

inputParams.label: Omit Metadata Keys
inputParams.name: omitMetadataKeys
inputParams.description: Each document loader comes with a default set of metadata keys that are extracted from the document. You can use this field to omit some of the default metadata keys. The value should be a list of keys, seperated by comma. Use * to omit all metadata keys execept the ones you specify in the Additional Metadata field

outputAnchors.description: Array of document objects containing metadata and pageContent

data.description:
Split documents recursively by different characters - starting with "

", then "
", then " "

inputParams.label: Chunk Size
inputParams.name: chunkSize
inputParams.description: Number of characters in each chunk. Default is 1000.

inputParams.label: Chunk Overlap
inputParams.name: chunkOverlap
inputParams.description: Number of characters to overlap between chunks. Default is 200.

inputParams.label: Custom Separators
inputParams.name: separators
inputParams.description: Array of custom separators to determine when to split the text, will override the default separators

data.label: Upstash Vector
data.name: upstash
data.description: Upsert data as embedding or string and perform similarity search with Upstash, the leading serverless data platform

inputParams.label: Connect Credential
inputParams.name: credential
inputParams.description: Necessary credentials for the HTTP connection

inputParams.label: File Upload
inputParams.name: fileUpload
inputParams.description: Allow file upload on the chat

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
**File Upload**

This allows file upload on the chat. Uploaded files will be upserted on the fly to the vector store.

**Note:**
- You can only turn on file upload for one vector store at a time.
- At least one Document Loader node should be connected to the document input.
- Document Loader should be file types like PDF, DOCX, TXT, etc.

**How it works**
- Uploaded files will have the metadata updated with the chatId.
- This will allow the file to be associated with the chatId.
- When querying, metadata will be filtered by chatId to retrieve files associated with the chatId.

inputParams.label: Top K
inputParams.name: topK
inputParams.description: Number of top results to fetch. Default to 4

inputAnchors.label: Record Manager
inputAnchors.name: recordManager
inputAnchors.description: Keep track of the record to prevent duplication

data.label: Postgres Record Manager
data.name: postgresRecordManager
data.description: Use Postgres to keep track of document writes into the vector databases

inputParams.label: SSL
inputParams.name: ssl
inputParams.description: Use SSL to connect to Postgres

inputParams.label: Cleanup
inputParams.name: cleanup
inputParams.description: Read more on the difference between different cleanup methods here

options.label: None
options.name: none
options.description: No clean up of old content

options.label: Incremental
options.name: incremental
options.description: Delete previous versions of the content if content of the source document has changed. Important!! SourceId Key must be specified and document metadata must contains the specified key

options.label: Full
options.name: full
options.description: Same as incremental, but if the source document has been deleted, it will be deleted from vector store as well, incremental mode will not.

inputParams.label: SourceId Key
inputParams.name: sourceIdKey
inputParams.description: Key used to get the true source of document, to be compared against the record. Document metadata must contains SourceId Key

outputAnchors.name: postgresRecordManager
outputAnchors.label: Postgres RecordManager
outputAnchors.description: Use Postgres to keep track of document writes into the vector databases

data.label: Google Gemini Embedding
data.name: googleGenerativeAiEmbeddings
data.description: Google Generative API to generate embeddings for a given text

inputParams.label: Connect Credential
inputParams.name: credential
inputParams.description: Google Generative AI credential.

inputParams.label: Task Type
inputParams.name: tasktype
inputParams.description: Type of task for which the embedding will be used

inputParams.label: Strip New Lines
inputParams.name: stripNewLines
inputParams.description: Remove new lines from input text before embedding to reduce token count

outputAnchors.name: googleGenerativeAiEmbeddings
outputAnchors.label: GoogleGenerativeAiEmbeddings
outputAnchors.description: Google Generative API to generate embeddings for a given text

data.label: Ollama Embedding
data.name: ollamaEmbedding
data.description: Generate embeddings for a given text using open source model on Ollama

inputParams.label: Number of GPU
inputParams.name: numGpu
inputParams.description: The number of layers to send to the GPU(s). On macOS it defaults to 1 to enable metal support, 0 to disable. Refer to docs for more details

inputParams.label: Number of Thread
inputParams.name: numThread
inputParams.description: Sets the number of threads to use during computation. By default, Ollama will detect this for optimal performance. It is recommended to set this value to the number of physical CPU cores your system has (as opposed to the logical number of cores). Refer to docs for more details

outputAnchors.name: ollamaEmbedding
outputAnchors.label: OllamaEmbeddings
outputAnchors.description: Generate embeddings for a given text using open source model on Ollama

data.label: Qdrant
data.name: qdrant
data.description: Upsert embedded data and perform similarity search upon query using Qdrant, a scalable open source vector database written in Rust

inputParams.label: Connect Credential
inputParams.name: credential
inputParams.description: Only needed when using Qdrant cloud hosted

inputParams.label: File Upload
inputParams.name: fileUpload
inputParams.description: Allow file upload on the chat

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
**File Upload**

This allows file upload on the chat. Uploaded files will be upserted on the fly to the vector store.

**Note:**
- You can only turn on file upload for one vector store at a time.
- At least one Document Loader node should be connected to the document input.
- Document Loader should be file types like PDF, DOCX, TXT, etc.

**How it works**
- Uploaded files will have the metadata updated with the chatId.
- This will allow the file to be associated with the chatId.
- When querying, metadata will be filtered by chatId to retrieve files associated with the chatId.

inputParams.label: Content Key
inputParams.name: contentPayloadKey
inputParams.description: The key for storing text. Default to `content`

inputParams.label: Metadata Key
inputParams.name: metadataPayloadKey
inputParams.description: The key for storing metadata. Default to `metadata`

inputParams.label: Upsert Batch Size
inputParams.name: batchSize
inputParams.description: Upsert in batches of size N

inputParams.label: Similarity
inputParams.name: qdrantSimilarity
inputParams.description: Similarity measure used in Qdrant.

inputParams.label: Additional Collection Cofiguration
inputParams.name: qdrantCollectionConfiguration
inputParams.description: Refer to collection docs for more reference

inputParams.label: Top K
inputParams.name: topK
inputParams.description: Number of top results to fetch. Default to 4

inputParams.label: Qdrant Search Filter
inputParams.name: qdrantFilter
inputParams.description: Only return points which satisfy the conditions

inputAnchors.name: recordManager
inputAnchors.description: Keep track of the record to prevent duplication
